description_long: This is a large, shallow ceramic bowl with a wide, open mouth and a slightly raised foot. The exterior is adorned with a band of elegant calligraphic script encircling the upper portion, rendered in dark brown or black against a creamy white background. The interior features a frieze of blue and brown painted figures, likely horsemen, set against a pale backdrop, with a decorative blue border along the rim. The bowl's surface appears slightly textured, with hand-painted motifs and a matte finish. The overall style suggests a historical or traditional design, possibly from the Middle East or Central Asia. The bowl is empty and shows no visible cracks or chips, indicating it is in good condition.
description: A large, shallow ceramic bowl with calligraphic script on the outside and painted horsemen and blue border on the inside, finished in cream, blue, and brown tones.
description_view_0: This view highlights the calligraphic script on the exterior and a clear section of the interior frieze with several horsemen and a blue border.
description_view_1: This angle shows more of the interior horsemen motif and the continuation of the calligraphic band, with the blue rim pattern prominent.
description_view_2: This side displays a different segment of the script and interior figures, with the blue border and painted motifs clearly visible.
description_view_3: This view reveals another section of the interior with horsemen and script, and the blue border is consistent around the rim.
attribute_location: dining table, ceremonial space, or display shelf
attribute_decorative_patterns: Calligraphic script encircling the exterior, painted horsemen and blue border on the interior rim, with additional blue and brown motifs.
